title: MCP Architecture, Negotiation, and Trust Boundaries · Design Defense
summary: Trace one tool call across host, client, server, and external system. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for an MCP architecture and threat-boundary diagram. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish host, per-server client, server, model, user, and external service responsibilities.
Trace initialization, capability negotiation, requests, responses, notifications, and errors.
Advertise and negotiate only implemented protocol capabilities.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Use a changed quiz case for agent ext mcp architecture that tests this competency: Target a named stable specification and isolate release-candidate changes.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Spec Versioning: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Use a changed quiz case for agent ext mcp architecture that tests this competency: Target a named stable specification and isolate release-candidate changes.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
steps.why: Retrieving Spec Versioning through the concrete an MCP architecture and threat-boundary diagram case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Target a named stable specification and isolate release-candidate changes.
